tagline: Estimate your VO2 max from running performance
keywords: VO2 max, aerobic fitness, cardio fitness, running
calcFn: var speed = distance / (time / 60); var vo2max = 3.5 + (speed * 5.5); var rating = vo2max >= 50 ? 'Excellent' : vo2max >= 40 ? 'Good' : vo2max >= 30 ? 'Average' : 'Below Average'; return {vo2max: fmt(vo2max,1), rating: rating};

faq.q: What is a good VO2 max?
faq.a: 40-50 ml/kg/min is good for adults. Elite athletes reach 60-80.

faq.q: How can I improve VO2 max?
faq.a: Interval training and consistent aerobic exercise improve VO2 max.

tagline: Estimate your biological age based on lifestyle factors
keywords: biological age, longevity, health age, aging
calcFn: var bioAge = chronAge; bioAge -= Math.min(exercise * 0.5, 5); bioAge += (sleep < 6 || sleep > 9) ? 3 : (sleep < 7 ? 1 : -1); bioAge += smoking === 'Yes' ? 8 : 0; bioAge += bmi > 30 ? 4 : bmi > 25 ? 2 : bmi < 18.5 ? 2 : -1; var difference = bioAge - chronAge; return {bioAge: fmt(bioAge,0), difference: (difference >= 0 ? '+' : '') + fmt(difference,0)};

faq.q: What is biological age?
faq.a: An estimate of how old your body functions, which may differ from your calendar age.

faq.q: Can I lower my biological age?
faq.a: Yes, through exercise, quality sleep, healthy weight, and not smoking.

formulas.name: Bio Age
formulas.formula: Bio Age = Chrono Age ± Lifestyle Adjustments
formulas.explanation: Adjust chronological age based on lifestyle factors.

tagline: Rate your sleep quality based on key metrics
keywords: sleep quality, sleep score, sleep health, insomnia
calcFn: var durationScore = duration >= 7 && duration <= 9 ? 30 : duration >= 6 ? 20 : 10; var latencyScore = latency <= 15 ? 25 : latency <= 30 ? 15 : 5; var wakeScore = wakeups === 0 ? 20 : wakeups <= 1 ? 15 : wakeups <= 3 ? 8 : 3; var restScore = restedness * 2.5; var score = durationScore + latencyScore + wakeScore + restScore; var rating = score >= 80 ? 'Excellent' : score >= 60 ? 'Good' : score >= 40 ? 'Fair' : 'Poor'; return {score: fmt(score,0), rating: rating};

faq.q: What is good sleep quality?
faq.a: Falling asleep in under 20 min, sleeping 7-9 hours, waking 0-1 times.

faq.q: How to improve sleep?
faq.a: Consistent schedule, cool dark room, no screens 1hr before bed.

formulas.name: Sleep Score
formulas.formula: Score = Duration + Latency + Wakeup + Restedness points
formulas.explanation: Composite score from four sleep quality components.

tagline: Calculate a personalized daily step target
keywords: step goal, walking, daily steps, fitness tracker
calcFn: var targets = {'General Health': 8000, 'Weight Loss': 10000, 'Heart Health': 9000, 'Longevity': 7500}; var base = targets[goal] || 8000; var ageMod = age > 65 ? -1500 : age > 50 ? -500 : 0; var targetSteps = base + ageMod; var miles = targetSteps / 2100; var calories = targetSteps * 0.04; return {targetSteps: fmt(targetSteps,0), miles: fmt(miles,1), calories: fmt(calories,0)};

faq.q: Is 10,000 steps really necessary?
faq.a: Research shows significant health benefits at 7,000-8,000 steps, with diminishing returns after.

faq.q: How far is 10,000 steps?
faq.a: About 4.5-5 miles depending on stride length.

tagline: Calculate calories your body burns at rest
keywords: RMR, resting metabolic rate, basal metabolism, calories at rest
calcFn: var weightKg = weight * 0.453592; var heightCm = height * 2.54; var rmr = sex === 'Male' ? (10 * weightKg) + (6.25 * heightCm) - (5 * age) + 5 : (10 * weightKg) + (6.25 * heightCm) - (5 * age) - 161; var hourly = rmr / 24; return {rmr: fmt(rmr,0), hourly: fmt(hourly,1)};

faq.q: What is RMR?
faq.a: The calories your body burns at complete rest to maintain basic functions like breathing and circulation.

faq.q: How is RMR different from BMR?
faq.a: BMR is measured in stricter conditions. RMR is slightly higher and more practical to measure.

formulas.name: Mifflin-St Jeor
formulas.explanation: The most accurate RMR estimation formula.

tagline: Calculate your daily water intake requirements
keywords: water intake, hydration, daily water, fluid needs
calcFn: var base = weight * 0.5; var exerciseOz = exercise / 30 * 12; var climateMod = {'Temperate': 1, 'Hot/Humid': 1.2, 'Cold/Dry': 1.1, 'High Altitude': 1.15}; var ounces = (base + exerciseOz) * (climateMod[climate] || 1); var liters = ounces * 0.0296; var glasses = Math.ceil(ounces / 8); return {ounces: fmt(ounces,0), liters: fmt(liters,1), glasses: fmt(glasses,0)};

faq.q: How much water should I drink daily?
faq.a: A good baseline is half your body weight in ounces, adjusted for activity and climate.

faq.q: Does coffee count toward hydration?
faq.a: Yes, moderate caffeine intake still contributes to hydration despite mild diuretic effects.

formulas.name: Daily Water
formulas.explanation: Base hydration plus activity and climate adjustments.

tagline: Calculate the cost to offset your carbon footprint
keywords: carbon offset, carbon footprint, carbon credits, emissions
calcFn: var drivingTons = annualMiles * 0.000404; var flightTons = flights * 1.6; var electricTons = homeKwh * 12 * 0.000417; var totalTons = drivingTons + flightTons + electricTons; var offsetCost = totalTons * 15; var premiumCost = totalTons * 30; return {totalTons: fmt(totalTons,1), offsetCost: dollar(offsetCost), premiumCost: dollar(premiumCost)};

faq.q: How much does it cost to offset carbon?
faq.a: Voluntary carbon offsets range from $10-50 per ton of CO2.

faq.q: What is the average American carbon footprint?
faq.a: About 16 tons of CO2 per year, compared to a global average of 4 tons.

formulas.name: Driving Emissions
formulas.explanation: Average car emits about 404g CO2 per mile.

tagline: Estimate property risk from projected sea level rise
keywords: sea level rise, coastal flooding, climate risk, property elevation
calcFn: var annualRise = 0.013; var projected = (annualRise * timeframe) + (timeframe > 30 ? timeframe * 0.005 : 0); var safetyMargin = elevation - projected; var riskLevel = safetyMargin < 0 ? 'Extreme' : safetyMargin < 3 ? 'High' : safetyMargin < 10 ? 'Moderate' : 'Low'; return {projected: fmt(projected,1), riskLevel: riskLevel, safetyMargin: fmt(safetyMargin,1)};

faq.q: How fast is sea level rising?
faq.a: Currently about 3.6mm (0.14 inches) per year, accelerating over time.

faq.q: Should I worry about my coastal property?
faq.a: Properties under 10ft elevation within 1 mile of coast face increasing risk over 30+ years.

formulas.name: Projected Rise
formulas.formula: Rise = Annual Rate × Years + Acceleration Factor
formulas.explanation: Linear rise plus acceleration for longer timeframes.

tagline: Model a renewable energy portfolio for your needs
keywords: renewable energy, solar, wind, energy mix, clean energy
calcFn: var total = solarPct + windPct + hydroPct; var solarKwh = annualKwh * (solarPct / Math.max(total,1)); var windKwh = annualKwh * (windPct / Math.max(total,1)); var hydroKwh = annualKwh * (hydroPct / Math.max(total,1)); var co2Saved = annualKwh * 0.000907; return {solarKwh: fmt(solarKwh,0), windKwh: fmt(windKwh,0), hydroKwh: fmt(hydroKwh,0), co2Saved: fmt(co2Saved,1)};

faq.q: What is the best renewable energy mix?
faq.a: Depends on location. Solar dominates in sunny areas, wind in plains and coasts.

faq.q: How much CO2 does renewable energy save?
faq.a: About 0.9 tons per 1000 kWh compared to coal-fired electricity.

formulas.name: CO2 Saved
formulas.explanation: Average coal plant emits 907g CO2 per kWh.

tagline: Calculate how many trees to plant to offset your carbon
keywords: tree planting, carbon sequestration, offset, reforestation
calcFn: var rates = {'Deciduous (oak, maple)': 0.048, 'Conifer (pine, spruce)': 0.04, 'Tropical (fast-growing)': 0.065}; var rate = rates[treeType] || 0.048; var totalAbsorption = rate * years; var treesNeeded = Math.ceil(tons / totalAbsorption); var acres = treesNeeded / 400; var cost = treesNeeded * 3; return {treesNeeded: fmt(treesNeeded,0), acres: fmt(acres,1), cost: dollar(cost)};

faq.q: How much CO2 does one tree absorb?
faq.a: A mature tree absorbs about 48 lbs (0.022 tons) of CO2 per year on average.

faq.q: How many trees offset a person?
faq.a: About 700-800 trees to offset the average American's 16 tons/year footprint.

formulas.name: Trees Needed
formulas.explanation: Total offset divided by per-tree lifetime absorption.

tagline: Calculate water savings from conservation measures
keywords: water conservation, water savings, water usage, drought
calcFn: var showerGal = showerMin * 2.5 * 30; var flushGal = flushes * 3.5 * 30; var laundryGal = laundryLoads * 30 * 4.33; var lawnGal = lawnMin * 12 * 4.33; var currentGal = showerGal + flushGal + laundryGal + lawnGal; var efficientShower = showerMin * 1.5 * 30; var efficientFlush = flushes * 1.6 * 30; var efficientLaundry = laundryLoads * 15 * 4.33; var efficientLawn = lawnMin * 8 * 4.33; var efficientTotal = efficientShower + efficientFlush + efficientLaundry + efficientLawn; var savingsGal = currentGal - efficientTotal; var savingsCost = savingsGal * 0.005; return {currentGal: fmt(currentGal,0), savingsGal: fmt(savingsGal,0), savingsCost: dollar(savingsCost)};

faq.q: How much water does the average household use?
faq.a: About 300 gallons per day or 9,000 gallons per month.

faq.q: What saves the most water?
faq.a: Low-flow toilets, shorter showers, and efficient irrigation save the most.

formulas.name: Shower Savings
formulas.explanation: Flow rate reduction times daily usage.

tagline: Compare emissions from electric vs gas vehicles
keywords: EV emissions, electric car, gas car, carbon comparison
calcFn: var gasTons = (annualMiles / gasMpg) * 8.887 / 2000; var evKwh = annualMiles * evEfficiency; var evTons = (evKwh * gridCarbon) / 1000000; var savings = gasTons - evTons; return {gasTons: fmt(gasTons,1), evTons: fmt(evTons,1), savings: fmt(savings,1)};

faq.q: Are EVs really cleaner?
faq.a: Yes, even on coal-heavy grids EVs produce fewer emissions than gas cars over their lifetime.

faq.q: What affects EV emissions?
faq.a: Your local grid's energy mix is the biggest factor. Renewable grids make EVs near-zero emission.

formulas.name: Gas Emissions
formulas.explanation: Each gallon of gas produces 8.887 kg of CO2.

tagline: Estimate legal fees based on case type and complexity
keywords: attorney fees, legal cost, lawyer fees, legal estimate
calcFn: var baseRates = {'Simple Contract': 250, 'Personal Injury': 0, 'Divorce': 300, 'Criminal Defense': 350, 'Business Litigation': 400, 'Estate Planning': 275}; var hours = {'Simple Contract': 10, 'Personal Injury': 0, 'Divorce': 20, 'Criminal Defense': 30, 'Business Litigation': 50, 'Estate Planning': 8}; var complexMult = {'Simple': 0.8, 'Moderate': 1, 'Complex': 1.8}; var regionMult = {'Rural': 0.7, 'Suburban': 0.9, 'Urban': 1.1, 'Major Metro': 1.4}; var rate = baseRates[caseType] * (regionMult[region] || 1); var totalHours = hours[caseType] * (complexMult[complexity] || 1); var totalEstimate = caseType === 'Personal Injury' ? 0 : rate * totalHours; var retainer = Math.min(totalEstimate * 0.3, 10000); var hourlyRate = rate; if (caseType === 'Personal Injury') { hourlyRate = 0; totalEstimate = 0; retainer = 0; } return {hourlyRate: caseType === 'Personal Injury' ? 'Contingency (33%)' : dollar(hourlyRate), totalEstimate: caseType === 'Personal Injury' ? 'Contingency fee - no upfront cost' : dollar(totalEstimate), retainer: caseType === 'Personal Injury' ? 'None' : dollar(retainer)};

faq.q: How much do lawyers cost?
faq.a: Rates range from $150/hr in rural areas to $500+/hr in major metros.

faq.q: What is a retainer?
faq.a: An upfront deposit against which the attorney bills their hourly rate.

formulas.name: Total Estimate
formulas.formula: Total = Hourly Rate × Hours × Complexity × Region
formulas.explanation: Base rate adjusted for complexity and geographic market.

tagline: Determine if your case qualifies for small claims court
keywords: small claims, court limit, filing fee, legal dispute
calcFn: var limits = {'$5,000': 5000, '$7,500': 7500, '$10,000': 10000, '$12,000': 12000, '$15,000': 15000, '$25,000': 25000}; var limit = limits[state] || 10000; var qualifies = amount <= limit ? 'Yes' : 'No - exceeds limit'; var filingFee = amount <= 2500 ? 50 : amount <= 5000 ? 75 : amount <= 10000 ? 100 : 150; var netRecovery = amount - filingFee; return {qualifies: qualifies, filingFee: dollar(filingFee), netRecovery: dollar(netRecovery)};

faq.q: What is the small claims limit?
faq.a: Varies by state from $2,500 to $25,000. Most states allow $5,000-$10,000.

faq.q: Do I need a lawyer?
faq.a: No, small claims court is designed for self-representation.

formulas.name: Net Recovery
formulas.explanation: What you actually receive if you win the full amount.

tagline: Look up statute of limitations by case type
keywords: statute of limitations, legal deadline, filing deadline, SOL
calcFn: var sols = {'Written Contract': 6, 'Oral Contract': 4, 'Personal Injury': 3, 'Property Damage': 4, 'Medical Malpractice': 3, 'Fraud': 4, 'Debt Collection': 6}; var typicalSOL = sols[caseType] || 4; var timeLeft = typicalSOL - yearsAgo; var status = timeLeft <= 0 ? 'Likely Expired' : timeLeft <= 0.5 ? 'Urgent - Act Now' : timeLeft <= 1 ? 'Expiring Soon' : 'Within Limits'; return {typicalSOL: fmt(typicalSOL,0), timeLeft: fmt(Math.max(timeLeft,0),1), status: status};

faq.q: What is a statute of limitations?
faq.a: The legal deadline for filing a lawsuit. After it expires, you generally lose the right to sue.

faq.q: Can the SOL be extended?
faq.a: In some cases yes, such as discovery rule for fraud or tolling for minors.

formulas.name: Time Remaining
formulas.formula: Remaining = SOL Period - Years Since Incident
formulas.explanation: Subtract elapsed time from the applicable SOL.

tagline: Estimate the total cost of divorce proceedings
keywords: divorce cost, divorce attorney, divorce fees, separation
calcFn: var costs = {'Uncontested': [500,2500], 'Mediated': [3000,8000], 'Collaborative': [5000,15000], 'Contested (Moderate)': [10000,30000], 'Contested (Complex)': [25000,100000]}; var range = costs[type] || [5000,15000]; var childMult = children === 'Yes' ? 1.4 : 1; var assetMult = {'Simple': 1, 'Moderate': 1.3, 'Complex (business, property)': 1.8}; var mult = childMult * (assetMult[assets] || 1); var lowEstimate = range[0] * mult; var highEstimate = range[1] * mult; var timelines = {'Uncontested': 3, 'Mediated': 4, 'Collaborative': 6, 'Contested (Moderate)': 12, 'Contested (Complex)': 24}; var timeline = timelines[type] || 6; return {lowEstimate: dollar(lowEstimate), highEstimate: dollar(highEstimate), timeline: fmt(timeline,0)};

faq.q: How much does divorce cost?
faq.a: From $500 for simple uncontested to $100,000+ for complex contested cases.

faq.q: How to reduce divorce costs?
faq.a: Agree on as much as possible beforehand. Mediation is much cheaper than litigation.

formulas.name: Cost Range
formulas.formula: Cost = Base Range × Child Factor × Asset Factor
formulas.explanation: Base cost adjusted for custody and asset complexity.

tagline: Calculate the cost to register a trademark
keywords: trademark, USPTO, trademark filing, brand protection
calcFn: var perClass = filingType === 'TEAS Plus ($250/class)' ? 250 : 350; var filingFee = perClass * classes; var attorneyFee = attorney === 'Yes' ? 500 + (classes - 1) * 200 : 0; var totalCost = filingFee + attorneyFee; return {filingFee: dollar(filingFee), attorneyFee: dollar(attorneyFee), totalCost: dollar(totalCost)};

faq.q: How much does a trademark cost?
faq.a: USPTO filing is $250-350 per class. Attorney fees add $500-2000.

faq.q: How long does trademark registration take?
faq.a: Typically 8-12 months from filing to registration.

formulas.name: Total Cost
formulas.formula: Total = (Per Class Fee × Classes) + Attorney Fee
formulas.explanation: Government filing fee plus optional legal representation.

tagline: Estimate potential damages from NDA breach
calcFn: var severityMult = {'Minor (limited disclosure)': 0.1, 'Moderate (some competitive harm)': 0.3, 'Severe (public/competitor disclosure)': 0.7}; var mult = severityMult[breachSeverity] || 0.3; var estimatedDamages = liquidated > 0 ? liquidated : tradeSecretValue * mult; var legalCosts = Math.min(estimatedDamages * 0.15, 100000); return {estimatedDamages: dollar(estimatedDamages), legalCosts: dollar(legalCosts)};

faq.q: What are NDA damages?
faq.a: Can include actual damages, lost profits, unjust enrichment, and sometimes punitive damages.

faq.q: What are liquidated damages?
faq.a: A pre-agreed damage amount specified in the NDA contract.

formulas.name: Estimated Damages
formulas.formula: Damages = Trade Secret Value × Severity Factor (or Liquidated Amount)
formulas.explanation: Fraction of trade secret value based on breach severity.
